## [00:00 - 03:00]

So in the last video I walked you through several sales videos. Now I want to show you the sales page. In other words, what do you actually do with those sales videos? So there's a lot of different ways you can do this is just to have a sales video only. Very simple page, sales video with a buy button, and then that goes to a checkout page. And that's what I'm currently, as I record, is currently using in my process. And I'll show you an example. Then you could have a sales video where the sales video is on top of a full sales page, and then you have a buy button, and then you have a checkout page. So this is where you have basically a full sales video and a full sales letter. And then the other one is just to have a sales letter or sales page with no video that goes to a checkout page. So it's your choice of which to do here, and the real answer is to test to try different variations. But there's also the reality of creating a sales video is a lot of work and creating a sales letter or a sales page is a lot of work. And having both of them is twice as much work. And some people are going to feel more comfortable on video, and some people will feel more comfortable to sales letter. So you can make a choice on this, you can go all out, and you can test all the variations. But right now, personally, for me, I'm using a sales video. And then that basically has a buy button takes you to a checkout page. So the key things that we're going to look at is the idea of the headline and the subhead. And then what the message is, whether it's in a video or the sales letter, and remember, it needs to be about the transformation of someone's life is going to change. And then having the scarcity because having scarcity in your launch will literally double your results. And then having a clear call to action. So first, I'm going to show you the example for product launch formula that I'm currently using. So this is the example for my sales page. As you can see, it's super, super simple. Up at the top, we have a little bit of branding. We have a countdown timer. And I've mentioned this earlier, I only like to use a countdown timer in the last 36 hours or so, 36 to 48 hours. I think before that, if, if your countdown timer says that they've got five days, then they're going to look at that and say, Oh, I don't need to make a decision for five days. So I only like to introduce that countdown timer later in the launch in the last two days of the launch. So up there. So again, at the top, we've got some branding. I've got a countdown timer in the last two days. And then I have a sales video and that sales video. And then below it, there's the

link. Click here to register here. And so that link, we set that so only pops up once we get to the point in the video where I actually make the offer where I say, This is what you get. Now, one little thing we do is in the last 48 hours, again, at the same time that we put up that countdown timer is when we switch over and have that button pop up immediately when the video starts playing. And the idea behind that is that by that late in your launch, a lot of times people have already gone through your video, already looked at your video. And now they're coming back to buy.
